questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Reactions which take place in two or more steps are called as sequential or consecutive reactions. Consider the reaction given below $X \xrightarrow{K_1} Y \xrightarrow{K_2} Z$ where $\mathrm{K}_1 \neq \mathrm{K}_2$. The initial concentration of X is $\left[\mathrm{X}_0\right]$ and after time t the concentrations of $X, Y$ and $Z$ are respectively $X_t, Y_t$ and $Z_t$. Initially only $X$ is present in the reaction vessel and all reactions are elementary (use $\log 5=0.7$ and $e=2.7$ )Question: If the value of $\left[Y_t\right]$ reaches a maximum at time ' t ' and the values of $\mathrm{K}_1$ and $\mathrm{K}_2$ are $0.5 \mathrm{~min}^{-1}$ and $0.1 \mathrm{~min}^{-1}$ respectively and the initial concentration of $X$ is 1 M , then
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Reactions which take place in two or more steps are called as sequential or consecutive reactions. Consider the reaction given below $X \xrightarrow{K_1} Y \xrightarrow{K_2} Z$ where $\mathrm{K}_1 \neq \mathrm{K}_2$. The initial concentration of X is $\left[\mathrm{X}_0\right]$ and after time t the concentrations of $X, Y$ and $Z$ are respectively $X_t, Y_t$ and $Z_t$. Initially only $X$ is present in the reaction vessel and all reactions are elementary (use $\log 5=0.7$ and $e=2.7$ )Question: The value of time (in min ) when the concentration of $[\mathrm{Y}]$ reaches a maximum if the values of $\mathrm{K}_1$ and $\mathrm{K}_2$ are $0.1 \mathrm{~min}^{-1}$ and $0.5 \mathrm{~min}^{-1}$ respectively, is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: The following reaction was studied at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ in benzene solution containing 0.1 M pyridine. $$ \underset{(A)}{\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{OH}}+\underset{\text { }}{\left(\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5\right)_3 \mathrm{CCl} \longrightarrow} \longrightarrow\left(\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5\right)_3 \underset{\text { }}{\mathrm{COCH}_3}+\mathrm{HCl} $$ The following sets of data were observed.
Question: Rate law of above experiment is
questions.solution: $O$ comparing rates order w.r.t $A=2$, and w.r.t. $B=1$. Thus rate law $=K[A]^2[B]$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

Paragraph: The following reaction was studied at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ in benzene solution containing 0.1 M pyridine. $$ \underset{(A)}{\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{OH}}+\underset{\text { }}{\left(\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5\right)_3 \mathrm{CCl} \longrightarrow} \longrightarrow\left(\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5\right)_3 \underset{\text { }}{\mathrm{COCH}_3}+\mathrm{HCl} $$ The following sets of data were observed.
Question: Rates $\frac{\mathrm{d}[\mathrm{C}]}{\mathrm{dt}}$ in sets (I), II and III are respectively (in $\mathrm{M} \mathrm{min}^{-1}$ ) Rate constant of the above experiment is (in $\mathrm{L}^2 \mathrm{~m}^{-2} \mathrm{~min}^{-1}$ )
